questions.question: Differentiate between an array and a single variable. (2 marks)
keyPoints: An array can hold multiple values of the same type under one name, while a variable holds a single value.
Array elements are stored in contiguous memory locations and accessed via an index, whereas a variable is accessed directly by its name.

questions.question: Write a C program to read 10 integers into an array and then find and print the largest element. Explain your logic. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Declare an array of size 10 and a variable to hold the largest value.
Use a loop to read 10 integers from the user and store them in the array.
Initialize the 'largest' variable with the first element of the array (index 0).
Use another loop to traverse from the second element (index 1) to the last.
In each iteration, compare the current element with the 'largest' value. If the current element is larger, update 'largest'.
After the loop ends, print the value stored in 'largest'.
